title: parahancornia fasciculata
intro: Parahancornia fasciculata, also known as the "Kok Hock" plant, is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae, native to Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Parahancornia fasciculata include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system, making it a valuable herbal remedy in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of the plant can be found in various forms, such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of ailments and maintain overall well-being.
